question_text: A spring is described as having elastic properties. What does this mean?

options.label: A
options.text: The spring can be stretched but will not return to its original shape

options.label: B
options.text: The spring can be deformed by a force and will return to its original shape when the force is removed

options.label: C
options.text: The spring is made of rubber

options.label: D
options.text: The spring becomes stronger the more it is stretched

explanation: Elastic deformation means that when a force is applied and then removed, the object returns to its original shape and size. Springs are elastic materials as long as they are not stretched beyond their elastic limit.
